recap: BIDMAS sets the order for calculations: Brackets first, then Indices, then Division and Multiplication (left to right), then Addition and Subtraction (left to right).

steps: Brackets first — deal with the index inside: 2 squared = 4.
Division and multiplication have equal priority, so work left to right. Divide first: 20 / 5 = 4.
Left bracket — deal with the index first: 2 cubed = 8.
The result has an index applied to it: 5 squared = 25.

summary: BIDMAS: Brackets, Indices, Division/Multiplication (left to right), Addition/Subtraction (left to right).
Always resolve brackets first, including any indices or operations inside them.
Division and multiplication have equal priority — when both appear, work left to right, not always division first.
Addition and subtraction also have equal priority — again, work left to right.
